policy_statement: Budapest has set a target to reduce its greenhouse gas emissions by at least 40% by 2030 compared to the 2015 base year.
verbatim_text:
Accordingly, the Municipality of Budapest has set the target to reduce its emissions by 40% by 2030 compared to the base year of 2015.

Chart 2: The decarbonisation target of Budapest

2015: 6.11 million t CO2; 2030: -40%
section_header: 4.1.   REDUCTION OF THE EMISSION OF GREENHOUSE GASES (MITIGATION)
extraction_rationale: Quantified mitigation target with deadline and stated baseline year.
reasoning: The verbatim text contains an explicit, city-level mitigation outcome (“reduce its emissions by 40%”) with a clear deadline (“by 2030”) and a defined baseline (“base year of 2015”), plus supporting quantified context (“2015: 6.11 million t CO2; 2030: -40%”). This is an auditable, measurable commitment with spatial specificity (Municipality of Budapest). However, the excerpt does not explicitly state a binding legal mechanism (e.g., ordinance/mandate), so binding is marked “No”. Despite that, the language is firm (“has set the target to reduce”), not merely exploratory.
validation_reasoning: Quantifiable target: “reduce its emissions by 40%” and “2030: -40%”. Timeline: “by 2030”. Spatial specificity: “Municipality of Budapest” indicates the jurisdiction covered. Binding mechanism: no explicit legal/mandatory instrument (no ordinance/requirement/code change) is cited in the excerpt, so marked No. Weak language: not framed as ‘encourage/explore/aim’; it states “has set the target to reduce”, which is a clear commitment. Strong language: contains an outcome with hard numbers (“40% by 2030”). Therefore it meets the strict rule (timeline Yes + quantifiable target Yes; weak language No), so VALID.

policy_statement: By joining the Under 2 Coalition, Budapest committed to reduce greenhouse gas emissions by at least 80% by 2050 compared to 1990 levels and/or reduce annual per-capita emissions below 2 metric tons.
verbatim_text: By joining the Coalition, the parties undertake to reduce their emission of greenhouse gases by 80% as a minimum by 2050 compared to the values in 1990 and/or reduce their annual per capita emission below 2 metric tons.
section_header: 4.1.   REDUCTION OF THE EMISSION OF GREENHOUSE GASES (MITIGATION)
extraction_rationale: Long-term quantified commitment (percentage reduction and/or per-capita threshold) with a deadline year.
reasoning: The verbatim text contains a quantified, economy-wide GHG commitment with an explicit deadline: “reduce their emission of greenhouse gases by **80% as a minimum by 2050 compared to the values in 1990** and/or **reduce their annual per capita emission below 2 metric tons**.” This is measurable and time-bound. However, the commitment is framed as “undertake” via joining a coalition, which suggests a pledge rather than a clearly enforceable local legal instrument; thus binding mechanism is not evidenced in the excerpt. Spatial applicability is implicit to “the parties” (i.e., the joining jurisdiction), but the text does not explicitly state “Budapest” or “citywide” in the verbatim snippet.
validation_reasoning: Quantifiable target: Yes, because the text specifies measurable outcomes: “reduce their emission of greenhouse gases by 80% as a minimum” and “reduce their annual per capita emission below 2 metric tons.” Timeline: Yes, because it includes an explicit deadline: “by 2050.” Binding mechanism: No, because the excerpt describes a coalition pledge (“By joining the Coalition, the parties undertake to…”) and does not show an ordinance/mandate/code requirement. Spatial specificity: No, because the excerpt refers generically to “the parties” and does not explicitly name a geography (e.g., “Budapest,” “citywide,” or defined districts) within the verbatim text. Language strength: Strong language detected = Yes due to action verb with hard numbers (“undertake to reduce… by 2050… by 80%… and/or… below 2 metric tons”); Weak language detected = No (no ‘encourage/promote/explore/aim’ phrasing). STRICT VALID RULE: Passes because it is an auditable commitment with (has_timeline=Yes AND has_quantifiable_target=Yes), and weak_language_detected=No (binding mechanism not required in that case).

policy_statement: Aá0 measure: Conduct local climate modelling, city-level rainwater runoff and sewer system modelling, and a detailed vulnerability assessment for Budapest to parameterize adaptation measures, by 2022.
verbatim_text:
## Climate modelling and vulnerability assessment

Aá0

As part of this measure, forecasts need to be generated on a local level... Following climate forecasts, it is also necessary to create a city-level rainwater runoff and sewage system model...

It is also necessary to conduct a detailed vulnerability examination for the territory of Budapest...

Timeframe: 2022

It also reads as a necessity statement (“need to be generated”, “necessary to create”), but does not clearly establish a binding mechanism (ordinance/mandate). Under the strict rule for individual policies, absence of a quantifiable target prevents VALID.

Evidence: 'forecasts need to be generated on a local level', 'necessary to create a city-level rainwater runoff and sewage system model', 'conduct a detailed vulnerability examination'. Timeline: Yes. Evidence: 'Timeframe: 2022'. Binding mechanism: No. No ordinance/mandate/regulatory instrument is stated; phrases like 'need to be generated'/'necessary to' indicate intent but not enforceable authority. Spatial specificity: Yes. Evidence: 'for Budapest' and 'territory of Budapest'. Language strength: Strong language detected = No because there are no hard numbers tied to an enforceable requirement; Weak language detected = No because it is not phrased as 'encourage/explore/aim' but as necessity statements. STRICT VALID RULE fails because has_quantifiable_target != 'Yes' (gold standard not met).

policy_statement: Carry out thematic awareness-raising campaigns and informational activities via the Mayor’s Office and Budapest business organisations, focusing on reducing household energy consumption, with annual financing of HUF 100 million and 2030 energy/CO2 targets.

Thematic  awareness-raising  campaigns  and  informational  activities  through  the Mayor's  Office  and  the business  organisations  of  Budapest,  with  special  regard  to reducing the volume of household energy consumption

| Necessary financing | HUF 100 million per annum |
| ... |
| Target year: 2030 | Target year value: 432,496 MWh; 85,744 tCO 2 /annum |

It includes spatial specificity (“Budapest”) and a clear timeline (“Target year: 2030”). It also provides quantifiable outcome targets for that target year (“432,496 MWh” and “85,744 tCO2/annum”), which makes the policy measurable. However, the language is programmatic and not explicitly binding (no ordinance/requirement indicated), so enforceability is unclear; still, the gold-standard measurability criteria are satisfied.
validation_reasoning: Quantifiable target: The table specifies measurable outcomes: "Target year value: 432,496 MWh; 85,744 tCO2/annum". Timeline: The table provides an explicit deadline: "Target year: 2030". Binding mechanism: The text describes campaigns/activities but does not cite an enforceable mandate or legal requirement (no ordinance/regulation language), so 'No'. Spatial specificity: Implementation actors and geography are specified as "Mayor's Office" and "business organisations of Budapest" (Budapest). Weak language: Not framed as merely "encourage/explore/aim"; it states the activity will be carried out: "Thematic awareness-raising campaigns and informational activities...". Strong language: No explicit mandate/require/achieve phrasing with hard numbers; it is an activity description plus targets, so 'No'. STRICT VALID RULE: (1) Auditable commitment present (campaigns/activities to be conducted), (2) gold standard met because both timeline=Yes and quantifiable_target=Yes, (3) weak_language_detected=No, so passes -> VALID.

policy_statement: Create a sustainable, green and innovative procurement strategy to enforce climate protection aspects in the public procurement practice of the Mayor’s Office and municipal-owned companies, targeted for 2022.
verbatim_text:
## Promoting  climate  protection  aspects  in  the  ordering  of  public  services  and  public procurement processes

The purpose of the objective is to enforce climate protection aspects in the public procurement practice of the Mayor's Office and the companies owned by the capital city.

For  this  reason,  the Municipality of Budapest wishes to create its sustainable, green and innovative procurement strategy as soon as possible.

| Timeframe | 2022 |
section_header: Promoting climate protection aspects in the ordering of public services and public procurement processes
extraction_rationale: A specific municipal commitment to develop a named strategy (procurement strategy) with a stated purpose and timeframe (2022).
reasoning: This is an auditable commitment to *create* a procurement strategy, with an explicit timeframe (“2022”). However, it lacks any quantifiable outcome target (e.g., % of tenders with climate criteria, emissions reductions, number/value of contracts covered). The language is mostly aspirational (“wishes to create”, “as soon as possible”) and there is no binding mechanism described. Under the strict individual-policy rule, it fails the gold-standard requirement (timeline + quantifiable target).
validation_reasoning:
Quantifiable target: No. The text states an intent to "create its sustainable, green and innovative procurement strategy" but gives no measurable outcome (no % coverage, no emissions reduction, no number/value of procurements).
Timeline: Yes. The table specifies "Timeframe | 2022".
Binding mechanism: No. There is no ordinance/mandate/requirement; instead it says "wishes to create" and "as soon as possible."
Spatial specificity: Yes. Applies to "the Mayor's Office" and "the companies owned by the capital city" (Budapest).
Weak language: Yes. Phrases like "wishes to create" and "as soon as possible" indicate aspiration rather than enforceable commitment.
Strong language: No. No verbs like mandate/require/install/achieve with hard numbers.
STRICT VALID RULE: Fails gold standard because has_quantifiable_target='No' (even though timeline is present). Also weak language is present with no binding mechanism, reinforcing NON-SOUND.

policy_statement: Review the SECAP every two years and require responsible Mayor’s Office departments to report on progress every six months to the Chief Clerk (who reports directly to the Mayor).
verbatim_text: It is to be reviewed every 2 years to what extent the measures and objectives of the SECAP have been realized. It is  recommended  for  the  responsible  department s  of  the  Mayor's  Office  to  report  on  the  progress  achieved regarding the measures every six months to the Chief Clerk, who will then report to the Mayor directly.
extraction_rationale: Sets an explicit governance process with defined periodicity (biennial review and semiannual reporting), constituting an MRV/implementation policy.
reasoning: The text sets a monitoring/review cadence (every 2 years; every six months) but it is primarily procedural (MRV) and uses non-binding language (“recommended”). It does not include a quantifiable climate outcome target (e.g., emissions reduction, number of actions completed) tied to a deadline, nor does it create an enforceable requirement. Under the strict rule for individual policies, lacking a measurable outcome means it cannot be VALID even though timelines are present.
validation_reasoning:

Timeline: Yes. Evidence: 'reviewed every 2 years' and 'every six months'.
Binding mechanism: No. The text uses non-mandatory phrasing: 'It is recommended for the responsible departments... to report...'.
Spatial specificity: No. No explicit geography/jurisdiction is named in the excerpt (e.g., citywide, specific districts).
Weak language: Yes. Evidence: 'It is recommended...'.
Strong language: No. No enforceable verbs with hard outcome numbers (e.g., require/mandate + quantified deliverable) are present.
Strict valid rule check: Fails gold standard because has_quantifiable_target='No' (even though has_timeline='Yes'). Also weak language present without a binding mechanism.
